title: Fix optimizer evaluation finalization before candidate selection
description:
As an optimizer operator, I want procedure-created candidate evaluations to reach a durable terminal status before optimizer selection uses them, so that champion-promotion safeguards can trust ScoreVersion evaluation associations.

Feature: Optimizer evaluation finalization

Scenario: Successful candidate evaluation finalizes before selection
Given an optimizer-dispatched evaluation has a scoreVersionId association
When the evaluation subprocess exits successfully
Then the Evaluation record must be COMPLETED before the optimizer treats its metrics as usable

Scenario: Non-terminal candidate evaluation is rejected
Given an optimizer-dispatched evaluation remains RUNNING or FAILED
When the optimizer awaits the evaluation handle
Then the optimizer must treat the evaluation as failed and not select that candidate

Definition of Done:
- Evaluation.update fails loudly on missing mutation payloads and uses durable retries for terminal writes.
- execute_tactus evaluation handles fail when a subprocess exits before terminal Evaluation status.
- Feedback alignment optimizer rejects non-COMPLETED evaluation data.
- Tests cover update failures, handle finalization, optimizer rejection, and scoreVersionId association.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation. Confirmed scope is code-only: no data repair/backfill and no champion rollback. Initial investigation showed optimizer-created evaluations already carry scoreVersionId, but many remain RUNNING after all items are processed; the fix will target durable terminal writes and optimizer/handle rejection of non-terminal evaluations.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Added failing specs before production changes. Targeted run now fails on four intended gaps: missing Evaluation.update payload is silent, terminal writes do not request long-running retry, success-exit/non-terminal evaluation handles become completed_unknown, and optimizer YAML accepts non-COMPLETED evaluation data.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implementation is in place and verified. Test runs:
-  -> 144 passed.
-  -> 33 passed.
Behavior now fails fast when candidate evaluations are non-terminal, and terminal Evaluation writes use durable retry + raise on missing mutation payloads.
